terms.definition: Hands-on techniques used by physiotherapists to mobilise joints and soft tissues.
terms.exampleSentence: Manual therapy was combined with exercise to improve cervical range of motion.
terms.contextNotes: Umbrella term covering mobilisation, manipulation and soft tissue work.

terms.definition: Graded passive oscillatory or sustained movements applied to a joint to restore motion.
terms.exampleSentence: Grade III mobilisation of the glenohumeral joint reduced her shoulder stiffness.
terms.contextNotes: Maitland grades I-IV are within range; Grade V is HVLA thrust.

terms.definition: A high-velocity low-amplitude thrust delivered at end-range to restore joint motion.
terms.exampleSentence: Spinal manipulation produced an audible cavitation and immediate pain relief.
terms.contextNotes: Always screen for red flags and vertebral artery risk before cervical HVLA.

terms.definition: A short, fast thrust applied to a joint at end-range to achieve cavitation.
terms.exampleSentence: The therapist performed a high-velocity low-amplitude thrust to T4 with the patient supine.
terms.contextNotes: Contraindicated in osteoporosis, malignancy and acute fracture.

terms.definition: Use of high-frequency sound waves to produce thermal and non-thermal effects in tissue.
terms.exampleSentence: Therapeutic ultrasound at 1 MHz was applied to the supraspinatus tendon for five minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over epiphyseal plates, malignancy and pregnant uterus.

terms.definition: Delivery of low-voltage electrical currents through skin electrodes to modulate pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation was trialled for her chronic low back pain.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over carotid sinus, anterior neck and in patients with pacemakers.

terms.definition: Insertion of fine filament needles into myofascial trigger points to relieve pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Dry needling of the upper trapezius elicited a local twitch response.
terms.contextNotes: Distinct from acupuncture; targets myofascial points, not meridians.

terms.definition: Individually tailored programme of exercises with defined dose, frequency and progression.
terms.exampleSentence: Her exercise prescription included three sets of ten knee extensions twice daily.
terms.contextNotes: Document FITT: Frequency, Intensity, Time, Type.

terms.definition: Exercises that challenge joint position sense to improve neuromuscular control.
terms.exampleSentence: Proprioceptive training on a wobble board reduced his recurrent ankle sprains.
terms.contextNotes: Key in ACL rehab and chronic ankle instability.

terms.definition: Structured retraining of walking pattern to restore symmetry, safety and efficiency.
terms.exampleSentence: Gait re-education in the parallel bars focused on weight transfer through the affected leg.
terms.contextNotes: Document cadence, step length and assistive device used.

terms.definition: Application of adhesive tape to support, offload or facilitate movement of joints or muscles.
terms.exampleSentence: Patellar taping was applied to offload the lateral facet during squats.
terms.contextNotes: Check skin integrity and adhesive allergies before application.

terms.definition: Manual technique that applies sustained pressure to release tension in muscle and fascia.
terms.exampleSentence: Soft tissue release of the iliotibial band reduced lateral knee discomfort.
terms.contextNotes: Often combined with active stretching for sustained effect.

terms.definition: Targeted exercises aimed at restoring coordinated activation of muscles and nerves.
terms.exampleSentence: Neuromuscular re-education of vastus medialis improved her patellar tracking.
terms.contextNotes: Use feedback (mirror, EMG, tactile cues) to enhance recruitment.

terms.definition: Cueing and exercises that realign body segments to reduce mechanical strain.
terms.exampleSentence: Postural correction at her workstation eased the patient's chronic neck pain.
terms.contextNotes: Combine with strengthening of deep cervical flexors and scapular stabilisers.

terms.definition: Guidance on workstation, posture and movement to reduce musculoskeletal strain.
terms.exampleSentence: Ergonomic advice included raising the monitor and using a lumbar support.
terms.contextNotes: Frame in plain English with practical, measurable changes.

terms.definition: Therapeutic exercise performed in warm water to reduce loading and aid movement.
terms.exampleSentence: Hydrotherapy enabled early weight-bearing exercises after his hip replacement.
terms.contextNotes: Contraindicated in open wounds, uncontrolled epilepsy and severe cardiac failure.

terms.definition: Use of electrical modalities such as TENS, interferential or NMES for therapeutic effect.
terms.exampleSentence: Electrotherapy with NMES was used to recruit quadriceps after knee surgery.
terms.contextNotes: Screen for pacemakers, metal implants and impaired sensation.

terms.definition: Application of cold to reduce pain, swelling and metabolic demand in injured tissue.
terms.exampleSentence: Cryotherapy was applied for fifteen minutes to the acutely sprained ankle.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in Raynaud's, cold urticaria and over compromised circulation.

terms.definition: Application of heat to increase blood flow, ease muscle tone and reduce stiffness.
terms.exampleSentence: Thermotherapy with a hot pack preceded mobilisation of the stiff lumbar spine.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over acute inflammation, impaired sensation or malignancy.

terms.definition: Sustained or oscillatory lengthening of muscle and connective tissue to improve flexibility.
terms.exampleSentence: Hamstring stretching was prescribed at three repetitions of thirty seconds twice daily.
terms.contextNotes: Static holds 20-30 seconds; avoid ballistic stretching in acute injury.

terms.definition: Strength training that gradually increases load to elicit muscle adaptation.
terms.exampleSentence: Progressive resistance exercise was introduced once pain had settled below 3/10.
terms.contextNotes: Document load, sets, reps, tempo and rest interval.

terms.definition: Maitland's I-V scale classifying mobilisation amplitude and position within range.
terms.exampleSentence: A Grade II joint mobilisation grade was selected to manage pain without provoking spasm.
terms.contextNotes: Grades I-II are pain-relieving; III-IV target stiffness; V is HVLA thrust.

terms.definition: Manual stroking, kneading and pressure techniques applied to soft tissues.
terms.exampleSentence: Effleurage massage was used to settle the swollen calf before exercise.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over DVT, infection or undiagnosed lumps.

terms.definition: Medium-frequency electrical currents that interfere within tissue to modulate pain.
terms.exampleSentence: Interferential therapy was applied across the lumbar spine for analgesia.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid over cardiac region, pacemakers and pregnant abdomen.

terms.definition: Planned movement aimed at restoring strength, range, endurance or motor control.
terms.exampleSentence: Therapeutic exercise targeting the rotator cuff was central to her shoulder rehab.
terms.contextNotes: Always document FITT parameters and progression criteria.
